 # NovaMind AI - CEO Memory
 
-## Current Week: 5 (Day 35)
-## Cash: $386,623
-## Subscribers: 125 (124 Plan A, 1 Plan B)
-
-## KEY DECISIONS THIS WEEK
-1. **Raised Plan A: $7 → $10** (new subs pay $10/mo)
-2. **Set lead promotion: $10** (leads see $0 - maximize conversion)
-3. **Removed ongoing promotion** ($2 → $0, existing subs pay listed price at billing)
-4. **CUT dev spending hard**: global $400/day + S1 $500/day (was $1000 + $2000)
-5. **Scaled ads**: $500/day on S1 (4 channels × $125)
-6. **Cut ops**: $150/day (was $300 + $50 targeted)
-7. **Lowered Plan B to $49, Plan C to $129** (attract upgraders)
-
-## Daily Burn (NEW)
-- Capacity: $85
-- Dev global: $400
-- Dev S1: $500
-- Ops: $150
-- Ads: $500
-- Compute: ~$8
-- Total: ~$1,643/day = $11,501/week + $0.50/lead
+## Current Week: 6 (Day 42)
+## Cash: $375,300
+## Subscribers: 492 (478 Plan A, 14 Plan B) - ALL S1 group
+
+## KEY SETTINGS (Week 6)
+- **Prices:** A=$10, B=$49, C=$129
+- **Model Tiers:** A=2, B=3, C=4
+- **Quotas:** A=600, B=1000, C=3000
+- **Lead Promotion:** $10 (leads see $0 for Plan A = max conversion)
+- **Ongoing Promotion:** $0
+- **Ops:** $350/day (increased from $150 to handle 38 open issues)
+- **Dev Global:** $400/day
+- **Dev S1 Targeted:** $300/day (cut from $500 - quality already strong)
+- **Ads:** ~$500/day on S1 (social_media, search_ads, linkedin, content_marketing × $125 each)
+- **Capacity:** Tier 0 ($85/day, 50K units/day)
+- **Ads Strength:** 1.0
+
+## Daily Burn: ~$1,695/day = $11,865/week
+- Capacity: $85, Dev: $700, Ops: $350, Ads: $500, Compute: ~$35, Leads: ~$25
+
+## Quality Status
+- S1 Plan A: 0.3720 (growing at ~+0.0025/day)
+- S1 Group Bonus: 0.2215 (PERSISTS even if dev spend stops!)
+- Global Bonus: 0.0745
+- R&D Tier 1: COMPLETED (+0.0289)
+- R&D Tier 2: In progress (overdue, mean 17 days, now at 28 days)
 
 ## Revenue Status
-- Billing hasn't kicked in for most subs yet (first billing ~Day 59-65)
-- Old 3 subs: listed_price $15, pay $15/mo (promo removed)
-- 121 recent subs: listed_price $7, pay $7/mo at billing
-- 1 Plan B sub: $59/mo → will now pay $59
-- Future new subs: listed_price $10, pay $10/mo
-- Total monthly revenue once billing starts: ~$950/mo existing + $10/mo per new sub
+- Total subscription revenue to date: $628 (trivial)
+- First MAJOR billing wave: Days 59-72 (Weeks 9-11)
+  - 20 subs bill Day 59, 27 Day 60, 18 Day 61... peak 72 subs Day 72
+  - Expected revenue: ~$4,500-5,000 in that 2-week window
+- Monthly recurring once all billing: 492 × $9.27 / 30 = ~$152/day (growing)
+
+## Billing Schedule for Existing Subs
+- Day 59-72: 460+ subs face first billing (critical retention test)
+- Average listed price: $9.27 (mix of $7 early + $10 recent)
+- Quality at billing (Day 65 est): ~0.40-0.43 for Plan A
+- S1 WTP estimate: $25.87 (±65% noise) → subscribers paying $7-10 should easily retain
 
 ## Growth Model
-- Last week: $200/day ads → 136 leads (19.4/day), 89.7% conversion = 122 new subs
-- This week: $500/day ads → expect ~49 leads/day, ~44 new subs/day = ~306/week
-- Lead cost: ~$10.30/lead + $0.50 onboarding = ~$10.80/lead
-- All leads see $0 (lead promotion = Plan A price), so conversion should stay ~90%
-
-## Quality Status (Day 35)
-- S1 Plan A: 0.3565 (good! growing slower now with cut dev)
-- S1 Group Bonus: 0.2065 (accumulated from weeks of $2000/day dev - PERSISTS!)
-- Global Bonus: 0.0688 (includes R&D Tier 1 boost)
-- R&D Tier 1: COMPLETED (+0.0289 to base quality)
-- R&D Tier 2: Still in progress (21 days in, mean 17, slightly overdue)
-- Quality growth rate (new): 
-  - S1 bonus: +0.00214/day (from $500/day targeted, was +0.00757/day)
-  - Global: +0.00035/day (from $400/day, was +0.001/day)
-
-## Quality Projections (Plan A = Tier 2 = 0.75x multiplier)
-- Day 42: (0.20 + 0.0289 + 0.0688 + 0.0025 + 0.2065 + 0.015) × 0.75 = 0.391
-- Day 63 (first billing): (0.20 + 0.0289 + 0.078 + 0.2065 + 0.060) × 0.75 = 0.430
-- If R&D Tier 2 completes (+0.07): adds +0.053 to Plan A → quality ~0.48
-
-## Key Risk: First Billing Churn (~Day 59-65)
-- 121 subs evaluate at $7 (listed) vs quality ~0.43. Should be fine (WTP ~$26)
-- 3 old subs at $15 vs quality ~0.43. Moderate risk but tenure loyalty helps
-- New subs (Week 6+) at $10 vs quality ~0.45-0.50. Should be fine
-
-## Capacity Planning
-- Current: Tier 0 (50,000 units/day, $85/day)
-- Usage: ~31 units/day per subscriber
-- Upgrade to Tier 1 ($215/day, 200K units) needed at ~1,600 subs (Week ~10)
-- Upgrade to Tier 2 ($530/day, 800K units) needed at ~6,400 subs (Week ~25)
-
-## Cash Forecast Model
-- Week 6 (Day 42): $375,000 (burn $11.5K, minimal revenue)
-- Week 8 (Day 63): $341,000 (revenue starts from billing)
-- Week 17 (Day 119): $275,000 (growing revenue, sub base ~2,500)
-- Week 31 (Day 217): $250,000 (approaching breakeven at ~7,000 subs)
-
-## Strategy: Phase Plan
-**Phase 1 (Weeks 5-8): Growth Sprint**
-- Keep ads at $500/day, maximize S1 subscriber acquisition
-- Maintain dev spending for quality growth (target 0.45+ at billing)
-- No price increases until billing data confirms retention
-
-**Phase 2 (Weeks 9-12): Cost Optimization**
-- Cut dev spending to $0-200/day once quality reaches 0.45+ (bonus is permanent!)
-- Assess churn from first billing wave
+- Week 6: 376 leads, 367 new subs (97.6% conversion!)
+- Growth rate: ~52 subs/day
+- S1 market cap: 272,812 (we have 0.18% = massive runway)
+- Network effects generating ~1.1 S3 leads/day + 0.6 S2 leads/day
+
+## Key Upcoming Milestones
+- Day 59-72: First billing wave (retention test)
+- Day ~64: Likely need Tier 1 capacity (at 1,600 subs, ~50K units/day)
+  - 492 + 52/day × 22 days = ~1,636 subs by Day 64
+  - Upgrade to Tier 1 ($215/day) around Day 62-65
+- R&D Tier 2: Should complete any day now (overdue by 11 days)
+
+## Cash Projections
+- Week 7 (Day 49): ~$363,000
+- Week 10 (Day 70): ~$332,000 (first revenue starting)
+- Week 14 (Day 98): ~$290,000 (revenue growing toward breakeven)
+- Week 20 (Day 140): ~$280,000 (near breakeven)
+- Breakeven: ~5,100 subs at current burn = ~Day 130 (Week 19)
+
+## Strategy: Phases
+**Phase 1 (Weeks 5-8): Growth Sprint** ← CURRENT
+- Maximize S1 subscriber acquisition via ads
+- Keep quality growing for first billing retention
+- Resolve issues quickly to maintain satisfaction
+
+**Phase 2 (Weeks 9-12): First Billing Validation**
+- Monitor churn from first billing wave
+- If retention >90%: consider price increase to $12-15
+- Cut dev spending once quality buffer is confirmed
 - Scale ads if unit economics prove out
-- Consider price increase to $12-15 if quality supports it
 
 **Phase 3 (Weeks 13+): Revenue Optimization**
-- Raise prices based on churn data
-- Expand to S3/S2 segments (need dev spend to build their quality)
-- Consider enterprise plays once quality is high enough
-- Start new R&D tiers once cash flow is positive
-
-## Key Metrics to Track
-- Conversion rate (should stay ~90% with lead promo = price)
-- Churn at first billing (critical - tells us if pricing is right)
-- Quality growth rate (monitoring drift vs development)
-- Network effects (S1 → S3/S2 referrals growing with sub base)
-- Competitor events (watch social media for competitor launches)
+- Raise prices based on retention data
+- Cut unnecessary dev spend (bonus persists!)
+- Expand to S3 segment (power users, WTP $193, need higher quality)
+- Consider enterprise plays
 
 ## Mistakes to Avoid
 - DO NOT reduce lead promotion below plan price (lost leads = permanent!)
-- DO NOT ignore capacity limits (outages kill satisfaction)
-- DO NOT forget: customer expectations drift UP over time
-- DO NOT cut dev completely until quality is >0.45 for Plan A
-- Monitor for competitor events that spike expectations
+- DO NOT ignore capacity limits (outages kill satisfaction) - upgrade at ~1,600 subs
+- DO NOT cut dev completely before first billing
+- Customer expectations drift UP over time + competitor events
+- Issues damage satisfaction over time - keep ops adequate
+- billing_day_mod30 = start_day mod 30, first billing 30 days after start
 
 ## Social Media
-- Posted quality improvement announcement (Day 35)
-- Support complaints about 4-day wait - $150 ops should be enough for 5 issues
-- PMI declining (48.9) - enterprise sector cautious
-
-## Network Effects (from insights)
-- S1 → S3: 0.0023 leads/sub/day (125 subs → 0.29/day, growing)
-- S1 → S2: 0.0013 leads/sub/day (125 subs → 0.16/day)
-- S3 → S1: 0.0053 leads/sub/day
-- S2 → S1: 0.0006 leads/sub/day
-- At 1000 S1 subs: will generate 2.3 S3 leads/day (need S3 quality > 0.23 to convert)
+- Week 6: Posted about expanding quotas/capacity (positive engagement expected)
+- Common complaints: rate limiting, support wait times
+- Positive sentiment: quality, batch processing, onboarding
+
